records.ety: [From the root of spout or spit to eject from the mouth. Cf. Splutter.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sputtered (-trd); p. pr. & vb. n. Sputtering.]

defs: To spit, or to emit saliva from the mouth in small, scattered portions, as in rapid speaking.
To utter words hastily and indistinctly; to speak so rapidly as to emit saliva.
quotes: They could neither of them speak their rage, and so fell a sputtering at one another, like two roasting apples.

defs: To throw out anything, as little jets of steam, with a noise like that made by one sputtering.
quotes: Like the green wood . . . sputtering in the flame.

defs: To spit out hastily by quick, successive efforts, with a spluttering sound; to utter hastily and confusedly, without control over the organs of speech.
quotes: In the midst of caresses, and without the least pretended incitement, to sputter out the basest accusations.

defs: Moist matter thrown out in small detached particles; also, confused and hasty speech.
